programmes.show_description: Lee Mack hosts a special edition of the quiz in which the prize pot that is not won on one show is carried over to the next day's edition, meaning a potential jackpot of £500,000. What's more, anyone who makes it through to the final question automatically comes back to compete again the following day

Ant and Dec introduce further coverage as the campmates are split into two groups, which causes drama and two new famous faces join them. The first eating trial of the series sees the celebs battling it out to win dinner that evening, with the losers having to make do with rice and beans
News, sport and weather from across Scotland

Joel Dommett, Judi Love, Sam Thompson and GK Barry deceive unsuspecting members of the public as they take part in a fake love-themed show hosted by Matt and Emma Willis, who are also in on the whole thing and even get set their own mission in a hot tub. Plus, Olivia Attwood is the guest saboteur
Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features
A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions
The key headlines from the day so far

Stephen Mulhern hosts as contestants Alexander Armstrong, Natasha Hamilton and Jamie Laing to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice
David Dickinson is in Wolverhampton with his dealers Karen Dalmeny, James Layte, Mark Stevens and Fay Rutter
Stephen Mulhern hosts the exciting and iconic gameshow as one of his brave contestants faces the infamous Banker and tries to open those iconic 22 red boxes in the right order.

Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Christopher, Fenella, Shane and Elizabeth pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught
By the Scottish Conservative Party
A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world
Adil Ray hosts the game of words as journalist Cathy Newman and wordsmith Gyles Brandreth compete against Loose Women's Kaye Adams and Morning Live's Dr Ranj Singh for charity
Kyle finds Cain lying unconscious, while Kerry starts to have suspicions and Moira is concerned
Summer challenges Debbie's version of how Billy died, Will denies Sam's allegation that he's still seeing Megan, and Lisa and Carla leave Jodie in charge of Connie

While some embrace their new surroundings, one celebrity is far from impressed with their new living quarters. They aren't holding back, making their frustrations loud and clear to their fellow campmates. As night falls, there is a series of emotional and heartwarming revelations. But the peace is short-lived as the episode culminates in a showdown as one member from each team is selected by the other camp to face a bone-chilling trial that will test their courage to the limit. Ant and Dec host

Stephen Fry faces a grilling from a cast of autistic and learning-disabled interviewers. Did he take drugs in Buckingham Palace? Is he happy to be alive? As the questions come thick and fast, Stephen opens up about mental health, shame and self-criticism. There are probing questions about love, identity and belief, alongside moments of pure mischief and laughter - including an explosive question about his sexual preferences
A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents
Current affairs show tackling the issues behind the headlines from a Scottish perspective, with studio debate and analysis of major stories

programmes.show_name: Diet: Can You Eat Yourself Happy? - Tonight
programmes.show_description: For many of us, the food we eat to give ourselves a boost will often fall into the comfort food category. So what do experts say we should really be eating to improve our mood? Lara Lewington investigates.

programmes.show_name: Deal or No Deal: Banker's Best Battles
programmes.show_description: Stephen Mulhern hosts a look back at some of the most iconic Deal or No Deal episodes. Matthew and Alex are given their opportunity to open the iconic red boxes this time, but do they stay strong and resist the Banker's tempting offers in the hope of being rewarded with the 100,000-pound jackpot?
Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features
Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

Adil Ray hosts the game of words as Good Morning Britain's Kate Garraway and This Morning's Ashley James battle singers Toyah Willcox and Carol Decker to win big money for charity

programmes.show_name: STV Racing Live: Grand National Festival
programmes.show_description: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of day one from Aintree, where the feature races are the 2.55 Aintree Bowl and the 4.05 William Hill Aintree Hurdle, and races also take place at 1.45, 2.20, 3.30, 4.05 and 4.40. With analysis from AP McCoy, Ruby Walsh and Mick Fitzgerald, reports by Matt Chapman, Rishi Persad, Alice Plunkett and Luke Harvey, betting news from Brian Gleeson, Social Stable thoughts with Oli Bell and Sam Quek, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

All that stands between Ishan from Macclesfield, Marie from Essex, Taylor originally from Iowa, and Fiona from Gloucestershire and that cash prize is the small matter of one of the country's finest quizzers. Bradley Walsh hosts

Kerry wades in, while Robert gets an idea and Pollard is left with food for thought
Sarah sneaks round to see Todd while Theo is out, Jodie reveals her mischief to Gemma, Nick and Ben decide to get their sons together, and Ronnie offers Carl an olive branch

The two camps battle it out in an epic trial to decide who will win dinner that evening and who will be on the rice and beans. As Savannah scrub comes to an end, the campmates must take on a petrifying challenge that will push them to their limits. The camps finally come back together in a moment which sees joy and confrontation in equal measures. Ant and Dec host
